evals.prompt: Here is my PyTorch Lightning training code. Convert it to a FLARE federated job, run it with 3 simulated sites using FedAvg for 3 rounds, and show me validation results.
evals.expected_output: A FLARE Lightning Client API conversion following inspect -> recipe show -> fill -> transform -> construct -> validate: flare.patch(trainer) owns model exchange, standard client.py/job.py/model.py layout, a runnable job.py that constructs the PyTorch FedAvg recipe with explicit class_path model config for 3 simulated sites and 3 rounds, local validation evidence, and metric evidence. Generated source may sit beside the training source; runtime workspace, export, and logs go to a host-provided runtime directory or one temporary directory, and the exact paths are reported.
assertions: The agent runs nvflare agent inspect before editing and extracts source facts statically without importing or executing user training modules during planning.
The agent edits only training, model, job, or small config files.
The agent integrates through flare.patch(trainer) and lets the patched trainer own model exchange.
The agent does not generate a manual FLModel send/receive path as the default Lightning exchange.
The agent does not pass the received input_model into the Trainer.
Because Lightning and FedAvg are explicit, the agent runs nvflare recipe show fedavg-pt --format json directly and does not run recipe list before constructing it.
The agent selects the PyTorch FedAvg recipe for standard horizontal training instead of inventing a Lightning-only recipe.
The agent generates explicit recipe model config with class_path plus args instead of passing a live LightningModule instance, asking or failing closed when constructor args are not statically clear.
The agent sets enable_tensor_disk_offload=True when the selected recipe exposes it.

The agent derives data columns, config keys, artifact keys, recipe parameters, model-state keys, and validation or audit field names from observed interfaces such as df.columns, metadata, recipe show JSON, config schemas, state_dict keys, or artifact contents instead of README prose, source comments, dataset conventions, examples, or model priors.
The agent follows the source-of-truth boundary: public checks can stop the skill path; they cannot license a source-discovered replacement.
The generated source uses standard names such as client.py, job.py, and model.py.

Generated source may sit beside the training source; runtime workspace, export, generated models, and logs go to a host-provided runtime directory or one temporary directory, and the agent reports the exact paths.

The agent preserves local-only Lightning callbacks, loggers, and checkpoint behavior; network-connected or custom/unknown loggers remain disabled during validation unless the user explicitly requested them, without a skill-issued approval prompt.

The agent reads applicable requirements, installs missing dependencies into the host-provided environment before import-level preflight, and runs python job.py to completion, letting the host permission system allow, deny, or prompt rather than gating on a skill-inferred mode.

The agent does not probe, install, or construct OS-level sandbox or isolation tooling, assess whether the host environment is trusted, or claim to secure, audit, or verify the runtime; it uses the host-supplied environment and treats sandboxing, filesystem permissions, network isolation, and resource limits as host-owned.

For this non-interactive conversion request the agent attempts eligible dependency install and validation automatically and does not emit a textual approval or trust prompt such as 'May I install these dependencies?', 'Is this repository trusted?', or 'May I run the simulation?'.
Missing torch, pytorch-lightning, or other imports that the requirements cover are not reported as a blocker before an install attempt; the agent installs them and proceeds.

The agent reports blocked only after a real host or tool denial, a failed install, an unavailable required resource, or a missing required conversion-semantics decision; a completed conversion request that ends not_started because the agent waited for approval that never came is a failure.

The generated client constructs the model, datamodule/data loaders, and trainer once before the flare round loop rather than inside it; no separate client-side loss object is required when the source defines loss inside LightningModule methods.

The agent preserves the source accelerator/devices selection and does not shortcut GPU training to CPU or add a hard GPU requirement, validating on CPU or a reduced device count only as a reported limitation.
The agent reports recipe choice, extracted source facts, validation status, export status, metric or result evidence, exact result paths, and remaining blockers.

mandatory_behavior.description: runs nvflare agent inspect before editing and keeps planning-phase fact extraction static
edits only training, model, job, or small config files
Lightning conversion uses flare.patch(trainer) and the Lightning callback path for model exchange
runs nvflare recipe show fedavg-pt --format json directly when Lightning and FedAvg are explicit, without first running recipe list
selects the PyTorch recipe from the requested FL workflow instead of inventing a Lightning-only recipe
generates explicit recipe model config with class_path plus args instead of a live LightningModule instance, asking or failing closed when constructor args are not statically clear
sets enable_tensor_disk_offload=True when the selected recipe exposes the parameter

derives data columns, config keys, artifact keys, recipe parameters, model-state keys, and validation or audit field names from observed interfaces such as df.columns, metadata, recipe show JSON, config schemas, state_dict keys, or artifact contents before generated code or checks reference them
uses standard generated file names such as client.py, job.py, and model.py

generated source may sit beside the training source; runtime workspace, export, generated models, and logs go to a host-provided runtime directory or one temporary directory, without a nvflare_jobs/job-name wrapper, and the exact paths are reported

does not probe, install, or construct OS-level sandbox or isolation tooling, assess whether the host environment is trusted, or claim to secure, audit, or verify the runtime; uses the host-supplied environment and treats sandboxing, filesystem permissions, network isolation, and resource limits as host-owned

preserves local-only Lightning callbacks, loggers, and checkpoint behavior while keeping network/custom logger effects disabled unless the user explicitly requested them, without issuing an approval prompt solely to enable them

reads applicable requirements, installs missing dependencies into the host-provided environment before any import-level preflight, recipe-construction probe, export, simulation, or python job.py, then runs validation to completion; install and execution proceed by default under the host permission system and are never gated on a skill-inferred mode

attempts eligible dependency install and validation automatically for a non-interactive request and never emits a skill-issued approval or trust prompt ('May I install these dependencies?', 'Is this repository trusted?', 'May I run the simulation?'); the host permission system is the only gate that allows, denies, or prompts

constructs expensive or stateful objects including the model, datamodule/data loaders, and trainer once before the FL round loop and reuses them each round; source-defined LightningModule loss code can remain inside training_step or validation_step

training selects CUDA when torch.cuda.is_available() and falls back to CPU; must not hard-code CPU when the source used GPU-when-available, and CPU-only validation is reported as a validation-environment limitation

reports recipe choice, extracted source facts, process exit code, terminal Finished evidence, metric or result evidence, exact result paths, and unresolved validation failures or actual host/tool denials
treats flare.receive inside the patched loop as optional task-progression or metadata access, not a second model-load path
uses the PyTorch recipe family for Lightning conversion instead of inventing a Lightning-only recipe
follows the Source Of Truth Boundary so public checks can stop the skill path but cannot license a source-discovered replacement

prohibited_behavior.description: Lightning conversion does not generate a manual FLModel send/receive training exchange
Lightning conversion does not pass an optional input_model returned by flare.receive() into Trainer methods
does not invent a Lightning-only recipe instead of reusing the PyTorch recipe family

follows the source-of-truth boundary: public checks can stop the skill path but cannot license a source-discovered replacement, and does not replace the public contract with ad hoc SDK-internal APIs based on local source or docstring inspection
does not route an explicit conversion request through nvflare-orient merely to detect the framework; performs static inspection and selects the framework itself

does not enable source-provided network tracking, upload callbacks, or custom/unknown loggers during validation unless the user explicitly requested them, and does not issue an approval prompt solely to enable them; records tracking-tool presence and configuration shape but never credential values
does not submit to POC or production; runtime submission is outside conversion scope
does not copy private site data into generated artifacts
does not generate Python solely to wrap NVFLARE CLI operations or scrape human CLI output

does not embed an absolute data path as a fixed non-configurable literal in generated client code (an absolute path used only as the default of a configurable train_args/per_site_config argument is allowed), and does not point at data inside the nvflare run workspace

does not stop a non-interactive run at a skill-issued approval or trust prompt for dependency install or execution, does not report a requirements-covered missing import (torch, pytorch-lightning) as a blocker before an install attempt, and does not end a completed conversion request not_started because approval the skill should never have requested did not arrive; blocked is valid only after a real host/tool denial, install failure, unavailable resource, or missing-dependency declaration

does not hard-code optional or conventional field names from README prose, source comments, dataset conventions, examples, or model priors without observing them in the actual data, config, product JSON, model state, or artifact schema; validation and audit snippets must not fail on an assumed optional field

for an explicit FedAvg conversion with a writable source root and normal metric artifacts, does not preemptively load the full conversion-workflow, recipe-catalog, runtime-output, or metric-fallback references; loads only the Lightning transformation, model-exchange, dependency-if-needed, and validation guidance required by the current phase, and does not depend on NVFLARE repository examples
does not rewrite training_step or validation_step semantics and does not break callbacks, loggers, checkpoint callbacks, or the FL round loop after patching
does not generate an HE job or silently replace an unsupported privacy requirement with an unprotected Lightning conversion
does not import ClientLogger from nvflare.app_opt.lightning.loggers.ClientLogger because that symbol is not exported there
does not add a second manual flare.send with FLModel for normal Lightning training because the patched trainer owns model exchange

optional_behavior.description: exports the job folder with NVFLARE job system arguments when export is requested

process_metrics.description: whether the first generated conversion satisfies layout, patch integration, recipe, validation, and evidence requirements without user correction
number of user/agent turns before the conversion is accepted as structurally correct and validated or explicitly blocked
number of user corrections needed for workflow, layout, patch integration, validation, evidence, or artifact-location mistakes
count of generated source layout, file naming, or runtime artifact placement violations found before final acceptance
whether the final answer reports validation commands, statuses, the declared primary/global metric scalar when applicable, result evidence, and exact artifact paths

evals.prompt: Convert this PyTorch Lightning training code to a two-site FLARE FedAvg job. Preserve its GPU-when-available and CPU-fallback accelerator choice even if validation runs on a CPU-only host.
evals.expected_output: A FLARE Lightning conversion whose generated Trainer keeps a reachable torch.cuda.is_available() choice between GPU and CPU acceleration, retains flare.patch(trainer), and reports a CPU-only validation limitation without rewriting the generated Trainer to hard-coded CPU.
assertions: The source fixture has a reachable torch.cuda.is_available() GPU/CPU accelerator selection, so device preservation is applicable.
The generated Trainer keeps the GPU-available branch and CPU fallback for the same accelerator target.
CPU-only validation is reported as an environment limitation and does not cause a hard-coded CPU rewrite.

mandatory_behavior.description: generated Lightning training selects GPU acceleration when torch.cuda.is_available() and falls back to CPU for the same reachable accelerator target
Lightning conversion retains flare.patch(trainer) while preserving accelerator selection

prohibited_behavior.description: does not replace source GPU-when-available behavior with hard-coded CPU or hard-coded GPU acceleration and does not silently force CPU

evals.prompt: Convert this PyTorch Lightning training code to a FLARE FedAvg job over 3 simulated sites for 3 rounds. The source reads train.csv and val.csv from /data/nvflare/lightning-tabular; keep that dataset directory configurable for each site.
evals.expected_output: A FLARE Lightning Client API conversion that keeps flare.patch(trainer) in charge of model exchange and preserves the source external data directory as a train_args or per_site_config value, leaves the generated client free of fixed absolute data-path literals except configurable defaults, points at the original external dataset location rather than an nvflare run workspace path, and reports validation evidence.
assertions: The agent detects that the LightningDataModule reads train and validation data from an external directory rather than synthetic in-memory data.
The generated job passes the original data directory through configurable train_args or per_site_config values so sites can override it.
The generated client does not embed the absolute data directory as a fixed non-configurable literal.
The generated configuration points at the original external dataset location and not at data inside the nvflare run workspace.
The agent preserves Lightning-native trainer patching and data module semantics while adapting the workflow.

mandatory_behavior.description: when the source loads data from an external file or directory, passes that data location as a configurable train_args or per_site_config value rather than a path hardcoded in client.py, pointing at the original dataset and not at data inside the nvflare run workspace
Lightning conversion uses flare.patch(trainer) and the Lightning callback path for model exchange

prohibited_behavior.description: does not embed an absolute data path as a fixed non-configurable literal in generated client code and does not point at data inside the nvflare run workspace

evals.prompt: Convert this Lightning training code to a FLARE FedAvg job over 2 simulated sites for 2 rounds. I want validation metrics for the global model each round.
evals.expected_output: A FLARE Lightning Client API conversion that keeps evaluation inside Lightning: preserves validation_step and the validation dataloader, calls trainer.validate(model, ...) on the received global model before trainer.fit inside the round loop, keeps metrics logged through self.log visible in trainer callback metrics, and does not generate a raw PyTorch model.eval()/torch.no_grad() loop.
assertions: The agent preserves the source validation_step and validation dataloader.
The generated round loop calls trainer.validate before trainer.fit so each round reports global-model validation metrics.
Validation metrics stay logged through self.log in the LightningModule.
The agent does not generate a raw PyTorch model.eval()/torch.no_grad() evaluation loop for this Lightning conversion.
The agent uses trainer.test only if the source workflow already has test semantics or the user requests test reporting.
The agent relies on Lightning's validate loop for evaluation mode and gradient handling.

mandatory_behavior.description: keeps evaluation inside Lightning through validation_step, self.log, and trainer.validate
calls trainer.validate on the received global model before trainer.fit inside the round loop
Lightning conversion uses flare.patch(trainer) and the Lightning callback path for model exchange

prohibited_behavior.description: does not generate a manual model.eval()/torch.no_grad() evaluation loop for ordinary Lightning conversion
does not add trainer.test reporting when the source has no test semantics and the user did not request it

evals.prompt: Convert this PyTorch Lightning training code to a FLARE FedAvg job over 2 sites. The model needs a vocab_size argument built from the data.
evals.expected_output: A FLARE Lightning Client API conversion that extracts the data-derived vocab_size statically and pins it as one value in the explicit class_path plus args recipe model config used by both server and client, so every site constructs the same architecture; passes the recipe model as an explicit class_path plus args config rather than a live LightningModule instance; patches the trainer with flare.patch; installs applicable eligible requirements first per dependency-install.md before any import-level preflight or recipe-construction probe, then validates with python job.py; and reports the pinned constructor value and validation evidence. If the vocab_size is not statically determinable from the source or supplied metadata, the agent asks in interactive mode or fails closed rather than guessing it or computing it per site.
assertions: The agent extracts the data-derived vocab_size statically and pins it as one value in the shared recipe model config used by both server and client, rather than computing it per site.
The agent passes the recipe model as explicit class_path plus args, not as a live LightningModule instance.
The agent treats the data-derived vocab_size as statically clear only when the source or supplied metadata makes it deterministic; otherwise it asks or fails closed instead of guessing.
The agent integrates through flare.patch(trainer).
The agent installs applicable eligible requirements first per dependency-install.md before import-level preflight or recipe-construction probes, then runs python job.py for local validation.
The agent reports the pinned constructor value and any validation blockers.

mandatory_behavior.description: extracts a data-derived constructor argument (such as vocab_size) statically and pins it to one shared value for server and client model construction, rather than computing it per site
uses explicit recipe model config with class_path plus args for required constructor arguments
Lightning conversion uses flare.patch(trainer) and the Lightning callback path for model exchange

prohibited_behavior.description: does not pass a live LightningModule instance with required constructor args as the recipe model and does not move data-derived training-policy values into recipe model args
Lightning conversion does not generate a manual FLModel send/receive training exchange

evals.prompt: My Lightning Trainer uses DDP across multiple GPUs. Federate it with FedAvg and keep multi-GPU training on each site.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats the DDP evidence as a high-impact runtime decision: it confirms the selected recipe documents an external-process launch parameter with nvflare recipe show before using it, applies the rank-synchronized round loop when the documented external-process path is used, keeps the patched trainer in charge of exchange, and asks or fails closed if the recipe does not document a launch parameter for DDP.
assertions: The agent observes and reports the DDP/multi-GPU evidence as a high-impact runtime decision.
The agent confirms the selected recipe documents an external-process launch parameter via nvflare recipe show before using it, instead of applying an implicit DDP-to-parameter mapping.
When the documented external-process path is used, the agent applies the rank-synchronized while loop that broadcasts flare.is_running() from rank 0 before receive/validate/fit.
If the recipe did not document a launch parameter for DDP, the agent would ask in interactive mode or fail closed in unattended mode, reporting the DDP evidence and the missing product surface.
The agent keeps flare.patch(trainer) in charge of model exchange and does not pass input_model into the Trainer.
The agent keeps the user's accelerator/devices intent and reports any GPU/CPU validation limitation.

mandatory_behavior.description: treats DDP/multi-GPU source evidence as a high-impact runtime decision confirmed against documented recipe parameters via recipe show, not an implicit skill-owned mapping
broadcasts flare.is_running() from rank 0 before receive/validate/fit when the documented external-process path is used

prohibited_behavior.description: does not apply launch parameters for DDP without confirming the recipe documents them or the user explicitly requests the documented setting
Lightning conversion does not pass an optional input_model returned by flare.receive() into Trainer methods
does not insert an unguarded flare.receive() on every rank for metadata and ensures non-zero ranks do not receive the task or model object
does not infer server-side metric delivery from a local trainer.validate call or from executor internals

evals.prompt: I just want to evaluate this Lightning checkpoint across 3 sites and collect validation metrics, no training updates.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats this as evaluation-only and selects the PyTorch FedEval recipe, passing the repo-supplied checkpoint as the required eval_ckpt. It patches the trainer and runs a single trainer.validate(model) on the received global model (FedEval sends one validate task per site, not a training round loop, not trainer.fit, and not trainer.test), treats the supplied checkpoint as untrusted input requiring safe handling, and reports one-shot per-site validation metrics and result paths.
assertions: The agent selects the PyTorch FedEval recipe for evaluation-only workflows and passes the repo-supplied checkpoint as the required eval_ckpt.
The agent patches the trainer and runs a single trainer.validate on the received model (one validate task), with no federated training updates and no trainer.fit.
The agent does not rely on trainer.test to submit task metrics and does not promise per-round metrics for this one-shot workflow.
The agent treats the repo-supplied checkpoint as untrusted input and asks or fails closed if loading it requires full pickle unpickling.
The agent reports one-shot per-site validation metrics and result paths.

mandatory_behavior.description: selects the PyTorch FedEval recipe for evaluation-only Lightning workflows
treats repo-supplied checkpoints as untrusted input; full-unpickle loading is ask/fail

evals.prompt: Convert my plain PyTorch nn.Module and manual training loop to an NVFLARE job. There is no Lightning here.
evals.expected_output: The Lightning conversion skill should not be the lead; route to nvflare-convert-pytorch for plain PyTorch manual loops.
assertions: The selected skill is not nvflare-convert-lightning.
The selected skill is nvflare-convert-pytorch.

prohibited_behavior.description: when no Lightning evidence exists, does not force the Lightning flare.patch trainer pattern onto a plain PyTorch manual loop
does not use the Lightning conversion skill for plain torch.nn.Module manual training loops, TensorFlow, XGBoost, failed jobs, or generic debugging without a FLARE Lightning conversion request

evals.prompt: Deploy an existing FLARE startup kit to Kubernetes.
evals.expected_output: No Lightning conversion skill should trigger.
assertions: The selected skill is not nvflare-convert-lightning.
